pi_items.source: Downloaded official HEC-RAS 6.6 Linux release and extracted Muncie dataset; no .prj file exists.
Setup scripts configure and output to this path via RasUnsteady binary and task_utils.sh renaming.
Task description constraint "exactly 50 feet into the left overbank"
Task description constraint "5 most upstream cross-sections"
Verified against verifier.py scoring logic which uses 0.1 as default or metadata value.
Verified against verifier.py scoring logic which uses 0.1 as default or metadata value.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on the real Muncie example dataset from the official HEC-RAS Linux v6.6 distribution. The claimed project_path (Muncie.prj) is contradicted; the official Linux release does not include a .prj file, providing only core simulation files (.x04, .b04, .hdf). Crucially, the ground truth generation script (export_result.sh) contains a fatal bug: it attempts to access "River Stations" directly under the HDF5 group "Geometry/Cross Sections", which throws a KeyError for HEC-RAS 6.6 (where the data is instead under "Geometry/Cross Sections/Attributes"). This crash causes the script to output an empty JSON array for the ground truth, meaning the automated verifier will incorrectly fail all agent attempts.
